formal_model_command_table.locus: lines 437-454, Counter value target ranges and command order
formal_model_command_table.as_interpretation: This is the canonical command-buffer map adopted by ADR-0026.

formal_model_execution_anchor.summary: The formal model sketches 32 target/message cases, clears the buffer, places self-target messages in a self mailbox, places neighbor-target messages on output channels, and then processes self special messages.
formal_model_execution_anchor.as_gap: AS now models self mailbox state, command-message channel tokens, direct self-mailbox init execution, unsupported self-mailbox preservation, self-target init command-buffer dispatch, neighbor-target command-buffer delivery onto output channels, recipient-side init-family command-message input consumption, and the unsupported self-target non-init append boundary. It still does not execute non-init command-message inputs on recipient cells or execute self-target standard-signal/write-buffer commands.

legacy_divergences.locus: lines 206-222, buffer-process target and message cases
legacy_divergences.divergence: This differs from the formal command table, where 0-7 target self and offset 0 is standard-signal.
These legacy sketches define seven special messages and exclude standard-signal from that special-message list.

blockers.summary: AS can consume delivered init-family command-message inputs, but recipient cells still reject non-init command messages until standard-signal and write-buffer semantics are decided.

ADR-0058 records standard-signal command-token execution as blocked: ADR-0026 names formal offset 0, ordinary standard-signal processing is binary-input behavior, legacy special-message lists omit it, and RAA puts standard-signal at the final command case.
ADR-0057 records write-buf-zero and write-buf-one as blocked by source divergence across formal, RAA, SEMSIM, and FSMSIM witnesses.

allowed_next_slices: Select a multi-command recipient input conflict policy before accepting multiple simultaneous command-message tokens.
Add more source-status tests if another PRC source resolves the target, command-order, or standard-signal command-token divergence.
